36530915d191495cd8906c973edafe96b7c1997c: “Birds are afraid of curved branches”: The invisible resistance of the stock market
66bf8dce2b48b88fe7962ca8f54f3459126d2ae4: The article emphasizes that the "birds are afraid of bent branches" mentality is the major drag on the stock market, when geopolitical or policy shocks often only act as triggers for sell-offs. In March, VN-Index decreased in 14/21 sessions, on March 9 alone it lost more than 115 points, equivalent to 6.51%, in the context of Brent oil rising to 111 USD/barrel. A similar shock was the April 3 session, when the VN-Index dropped nearly 88 points, equivalent to 6.68%, after news that the US imposed a 46% reciprocal tax on Vietnam, then recovered when there was news of a 90-day tax delay. With individual investors accounting for more than 80% of transactions, the market easily falls into a cycle of sell-offs, technical pullbacks, FOMO and then losses, causing many people to buy at the top and sell at the bottom. The overall impact is negative in the short term on market sentiment and liquidity, but there are no specific stocks in the article to evaluate separately.

Approaching Basel III standards with new ratios The most important highlight in this draft is the addition of liquidity and capital management ratios according to Basel III standards.
